title: DiagInfect-AI
shortDescription: Multi-tenant SaaS that gives doctors an early, explainable AI infection-risk score from routine lab data.
fullDescription: DiagInfect-AI is a co-founded Algerian health-tech startup — a full-stack TypeScript platform that connects doctors, lab technicians, and hospital managers around an AI pipeline for early infectious-disease risk assessment. Hospitals self-onboard, invite staff by email, and work inside three role-specific dashboards. Doctors register patients and record vitals, lab technicians enter routine results (CRP, CBC, ESR, temperature), and the platform returns an infection-risk score with an explainable-AI breakdown of the factors behind it — before slow culture results come back. The system is fully multi-tenant, with organizations, subscription tiers, trial limits, and automatic overage billing. I lead the software: the entire full-stack build plus the AI-service integration layer, working alongside a biology teammate for clinical domain expertise and an AI supervisor for the prediction models.
problem: Bacterial infections are often diagnosed late because microbiological culture tests are slow and interpretation depends on scarce specialist expertise. The delay pushes clinicians toward late treatment and antibiotic overuse — a serious problem in low-resource hospitals that can't afford fast molecular diagnostics.
solution: A multi-tenant web platform where lab techs enter routine, low-cost results and doctors get an early infection-risk score with explainable factors — faster than culture and cheaper than molecular tests. Built with role-based dashboards for doctors, lab technicians, and hospital managers, plus organization onboarding, subscription enforcement, and usage/overage tracking for institutions.
impact: Shipped a complete multi-tenant SaaS: organization onboarding, email staff invitations, and three role-based dashboards (doctor, lab technician, hospital manager).
Built the full REST API across 8 domains with JWT auth (access + rotating refresh tokens, token blacklist), role-based access control, and subscription enforcement.
Implemented an explainable-AI prediction flow that surfaces the factors behind each infection-risk score, so clinicians can trust and interpret the result.
Designed a subscription engine with trial limits, upgrade prompts, and automatic overage billing for paid institutions.
Live on Vercel; the software platform is deployed while the clinical AI models are at proof-of-concept / pilot-validation stage (TRL 3–4).
